text: The surgeon begins searching for the appropriate site to make an incision in order to separate the target structure from the surrounding white tissue.
The surgeon employs a specialized technique to separate the outer layer of the cystic bag from its inner contents, which are contained within a plastic bag.
The surgeon cuts through the white outer layer of the cyst, creating an incision that allows for the peeling off of the cystic bag from the surrounding tissue.
The surgeon observes the upper part of the cystic structure, noting areas where the tissue appears more adherent and sticky.
The surgeon smoothly and quickly coats the area after successfully cutting the attached part of the cystic structure.
The surgeon cuts the remaining portion of the transparent coating surrounding the cyst, using precise surgical technique to remove the final layer.
The surgeon washes and cleans the surgical area, making a single cut on the white tissue to ensure it fits evenly within the operative field.
The surgeon removes the plastic bag from the surgical field, revealing the underlying structures.
